events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a flat/horizontal vent below flood rim in a commercial plumbing project. This is a code violation - vents must rise vertically or at least have proper slope, and a horizontal vent below the flood rim level of the fixture is non-compliant. Both IPC and UPC require vents to be above the flood rim level and properly sloped to drain back to the fixture.

Key defect: A horizontal/flat vent installed below the flood rim of a fixture. This is a clear code violation. Vents need to be above the flood rim level and should slope to drain moisture back. A flat horizontal vent below flood rim can allow sewer gas to enter and doesn't properly protect the trap seal.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (sewer gas, trap seal depletion, health hazard)
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant

"rationale": "Both IPC and UPC require fixture vents to be taken off the fixture drain and rise vertically to at least 6 inches above the flood rim level before turning horizontal, and horizontal vent sections must be graded to drain.
